action.reasoning: Observation: Eval works! mean_r2=0.303 with 3 epochs. ptend_q is the bottleneck at -0.07. Analysis: humidity tendencies are notoriously hard - they have heavy-tailed distributions. Need: more epochs, larger model, possibly per-dim normalization. With 23s/epoch, I can do 30-40 epochs in ~15-20 min. Decision: train longer (20 epochs) with current architecture as a strong baseline, then iterate on architecture if needed.
action.command: EPOCHS=20 LR=1e-3 HIDDEN=1024 DEPTH=8 python train.py
